acceptanceCriteria: A versioned evaluation manifest schema defines task categories, an initial 10-20 task set, a documented expansion path to 30-100 tasks, and an explicit calibration/holdout split per task
Evaluation reports use accepted changes as the denominator and show missing-data counts explicitly
A report can compare economy/standard/premium/frontier capability classes without treating configured relative cost as an invoice, price, or verified provider charge
Evaluation detects over-routing, under-routing, retry cost, and escaped defects where known, each with explicit numerators and denominators
Replay/shadow evaluation re-runs recorded routing inputs against proposed threshold or profile changes deterministically without invoking any model

Proposed rule changes are recorded as a calibration proposal artifact that includes evidence references, expected trade-offs, and rollback criteria, requires human review, and has no approval or enforcement effect
Holdout task results are never used to fit the same thresholds: replay/fitting against holdout tasks is refused or explicitly separated from calibration output
Documentation defines a recurring calibration owner and review interval, isolated worktree/fixture support for selected cross-model comparisons, and contamination-awareness rules
A sample report is produced entirely from checked-in synthetic fixtures with no live model invocation
constraints: Implement only Issue #16 scope; no changes outside allowed paths
Do not run live multi-model experiments or invoke any model from the CLI; evaluation is deterministic aggregation and replay over recorded evidence
Do not delete, weaken, or skip existing tests or configured checks
Additive schemas only: existing artifact schemas and their consumers must remain accepted unchanged
Configured relative cost remains an abstract comparison weight; never present it as a price, token count, or provider charge
Unknown usage, model identity, effort, and cost data stay unknown/null; never inferred or fabricated
Calibration proposals must not be auto-applied: no code path changes routing policy, thresholds, or profiles without human review
Do not claim statistical confidence from a tiny sample; reports must expose sample sizes
Fixtures must be synthetic, secret-free, and contain no absolute paths
Stop if protected paths, public API compatibility, or security boundaries need unexpected changes
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
